---
title: "Chapter 4 — Cross-region experiments"
subtitle: "Five-region transferability test for the DEEP-SEAM deviation-network architecture"
---

[← back to portfolio map](index.qmd)

## What this chapter is about

DEEP-SEAM (Luo et al. 2026, *Geosci. Model Dev.*) reported a 2-3×
top-k capture advantage over Random Forest on a Curnamona Province
REE dataset with seven known deposits. The natural follow-up
question: does that advantage transfer? To other deposit types? To
other REE provinces?

This chapter answers both. Across five independent regions covering
four deposit types and three continents, the deviation-network
architecture wins on exactly one — its tuning dataset. Classical
tree-based methods (Random Forest or gradient boosting) win on the
other four by 7 to 37 percentage points of top-1% capture.

## The five-region scorecard

| Region | Commodity | Positives | RF/GBM top-1% | DevNet top-1% | Winner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Curnamona REE (LOO, original tuning data) | REE | 7 | 0% | **43%** | DevNet (matches publication) |
| **US Western Carbonatite Belt (LOO)** | **REE carbonatite** | **14** | **85.7%** | **71.4%** | **RF** |
| Tanacross porphyry-Cu (OOF) | Cu-Mo | 45 | **22.2%** | 13.3% | RF |
| Arizona porphyry-Cu (OOF) | Cu | 191 | **23.0%** | 8.9% | RF |
| Lawley continental MVT (2-D blocked OOF) | Zn-Pb | 2,027 | **28.4%** | 0.0% | GBM |

The numbers are out-of-fold capture rates: fraction of held-out
positive cells in the top 1% of cells the model ranks most
prospective.

## What this means

The "DEEP-SEAM works on REE" reading of the published result is
falsified by row 2 of the table. The US Western Carbonatite Belt
test (8 deposits from Bear Lodge WY, Bearpaw MT, Elk Creek NE, Gem
Park CO, Iron Hill CO, North Fork ID, Ravalli MT, Wet Mountains CO;
14 positive cells after spatial join to Lawley's H3 cube; 408,293
unlabeled context cells; leave-one-out CV) sits in the **same
deposit family** as Curnamona — small positive set, distinctive
alkaline-intrusive host rock, characteristic geophysical signature.
DEEP-SEAM still loses to RF.

The geological explanation is clean. DEEP-SEAM's loss function
pushes positive cells far above a Gaussian-distributed reference
distribution. The assumption is that positive cells are feature-space
*anomalies* — points that look different from the bulk of the data.
That assumption fits Curnamona because the published preprocessing
pipeline (isometric log-ratio + PCA on geochem, GLCM textural
features on geophysics) compresses the data into something
approximately Gaussian with the seven deposits sitting in the tails.

It does not fit regional MPM on standard public-data feature stacks.
Lawley's MVT cube encodes prospectivity through proximity-to-feature
columns (distance to black shale, distance to passive margin,
distance to fault) and presence-flag dictionaries (sedimentary host,
igneous host). A prospective cell *fits the geological recipe*; it
is not an anomaly. The deviation loss looks for rare and finds the
recipe is common, so the deposits do not appear anomalous and the
model produces near-random ordering. RF can learn the recipe via
multivariate conditional splits; DevNet can't.

This is also strictly consistent with the [Chapter 3
audits](reproductions.qmd): the published Curnamona 86% top-2% capture
is measured under 0-D spatial blocking (no spatial isolation at
all). When the same architecture is evaluated under any form of
spatial isolation (LOO on a different REE province, OOF on
porphyry-Cu, 2-D blocked OOF on continental MVT), the advantage
disappears.

## What this implies for the field

The deep-anomaly-detection architectures (DEEP-SEAM, DevNet,
related deviation-loss methods) are being proposed in the recent
MPM literature as the next step beyond classical tree-based machine
learning. The five-region experiment says, concretely, that they are
not yet ready as a drop-in replacement for Random Forest or gradient
boosting on standard regional prospectivity workflows. They have a
specific niche — small-positive datasets with heavy upstream feature
decomposition that genuinely shapes the data into anomaly form — and
outside that niche they lose by a wide margin.

The architecture is not broken. It is matched to the wrong problem
shape for general regional MPM. Knowing where it works and where it
doesn't is more useful for an exploration team than a blanket claim
that the new method is better.

## Deeper notebooks

- [Cross-region four-cell deep dive](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/cross_region/four_cell.html) — the 2×2 controlled experiment design (DevNet/RF × DEEP-SEAM-style features / our raw features) across Curnamona / Tanacross / Arizona.
- [US Carbonatite REE within-niche transfer test](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/us_carbonatite_ree.html) — full writeup of the row-2 within-niche falsification.

[← back to portfolio map](index.qmd)
